divergence: The Black Death targeted only landlords
story_angle: human-interest profile of the one person unaffected by the great event and deeply annoyed about it
headline: Last Landlord Stands Alone, Seeks Retroactive Black Death for Fiscal Equity
deck: Daily ration log reveals 0.3 tenants per former unit, suggesting the market's never been better.
article:
Oglethorpe Grimswick, the last landlord of Hollowvale Tenement, submitted petition 47-B on rust-flaked parchment demanding the Black Death apply retroactively to his holdings. The form cites 1349 exemptions for bloodline purity and argues his survival amounts to an administrative oversight.

His ration log records 0.3 tenants per unit, which he converts into a shortfall of 6.7 corpses per square mile. "It is not merely revenue," Grimswick stated while gripping an empty purse. "It is precedent; absent tenants, I am reduced to counting dust motes."

Neighboring ruins now lease shade at twelve bullets per hour. The Town Council responded to the petition by adding it to an already overflowing stack of unclaimed ledgers.
author: Garnet Grimstone, Bureaucratic Disputes Correspondent

oped.title: Bring Back the Plague—But Make It Fair This Time
oped.author: Baron Eustace Evictmore, Emeritus Professor of Grievance Economics at the Wasteland Institute for Landlord Supremacy
oped.body: Grimswick’s demand is simple arithmetic. With 0.3 tenants per unit, surplus housing now mocks every unpaid ledger since 1349. A corrected plague should strike only those lacking deeds, plus any ancestor who wore the wrong hat during the first outbreak. I myself lost seventeen summer manors and now subsist on coupons. Fresh mortality would at least remind survivors that space once carried a price.

classifieds.text: For sale: three former landlord towers with 0.3 tenants each, free mutant rats included; no rent due to the Last One standing alone.
Help Wanted: plague courier to deliver retroactive Black Death spores to the final landlord for fiscal equity; bring your own wasteland spores, paid in daily rations.
Public Notice: the Last Landlord confesses his solitary stand has ruined the market and begs any survivor for one final targeted plague before year 3388.
Legal: filing retroactive claim against the Black Death for selective landlord culling; seeking co-plaintiffs who miss the old 1.0 tenant ratio.

panels.caption: Landlord scans ration log amid ruins: "0.3 tenants per unit. Retroactive Black Death now."
Wasteland clerk: "The plague only struck landlords. Can't touch tenants."
"Then classify me a tenant and close the books."

joke.setup: In the 3387 wasteland the last landlord unfurled his parchment before the empty dunes and demanded the Black Death strike retroactively for fiscal equity.
joke.punchline: Without ancestral corpses to inherit from, he explained, he had nothing left to evict but the wind itself.
joke.text: In the 3387 wasteland the last landlord unfurled his parchment before the empty dunes and demanded the Black Death strike retroactively for fiscal equity. Without ancestral corpses to inherit from, he explained, he had nothing left to evict but the wind itself.

sponsor_ads.headline: Retroactive Black Death Vials – Single Dose, Last Landlord Only
sponsor_ads.body: Apply the original 1349 strain to the final rent-collector and watch deeds dissolve into dust; one vial restores centuries of unpaid equity across the wastes.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Void if applied to anyone who ever paid rent.

sponsor_ads.headline: Landlord-Only Plague Insurance – Premiums Paid in Deeds
sponsor_ads.body: Secure your claim on abandoned towers before the last survivor dies of fiscal regret; payout delivered the moment retroactive symptoms manifest.
sponsor_ads.tagline: No claims honored for non-landlord casualties. Wasteland Act of 3387 applies.

sponsor_ads.headline: Public Bounty: One (1) Final Landlord, Slightly Alive
sponsor_ads.body: Collect the retroactive equity yourself—bring proof of death by Black Death or equivalent and receive title to every surviving structure in the irradiated boroughs.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Head must remain attached for verification. No partial credit for natural causes.

sponsor_ads.headline: Post-Landlord Real Estate Charms – 100% Ghost-Tenant Proof
sponsor_ads.body: Ward your new ruins against the vengeful spirit of the last man who ever tried to collect rent; each charm includes a fragment of his final ledger.
sponsor_ads.tagline: May cause mild hauntings of unpaid invoices. Not responsible for spectral audits.

stories.headline: Former Rental Ledgers Now Serve as Kindling After Final Audit
stories.deck: Zero rent receipts recorded for the 872nd consecutive season.
stories.article:
Assessors counted 1,204 vacant holdings whose only bidders were firewood merchants offering two bushels of root per shell. Tenants have converted interiors into shared hearths rather than pursue ownership.

The final exchange transferred a guild hall for one working water filter, ending any pretense of leaseholds.

stories.headline: Communal Plots Yield 12% More Without Oversight Tithes
stories.deck: Harvest tallies exclude any column for ground rent.
stories.article:
Seed once reserved for absent owners now stretches rotations nineteen days longer. Salt-root and ash-grain reached 3.4 measures per plot, the first surplus since the 3190s.

Boundary disputes have ceased; irrigation follows slope rather than old deeds.

stories.headline: Clergy Petition for Targeted Plague Return to Restore Balance
stories.deck: Prayers specify landlords only, with clauses excluding current occupants.
stories.article:
The Order of Final Reckoning filed forty-seven identical scrolls requesting the 1348 strain return solely for property holders. Services now observe a minute of silence for departed rent collectors.

Pew occupancy sits at 0.3 worshippers, matching the district tenant ratio without adjustment.

real_world_echo: obliquely mirrors 'Injury ends Williams' Wimbledon comeback' by framing the absence of affliction as the event that ruins the subject's accustomed position
